response:
<<Sorting by Length and Harmony>>

To organize the records within the `title` and `director` columns in a diminishing order based on their character length, and to handle cases where two titles or directors have the same length, you can use the `ORDER BY` clause with `LENGTH()` function in SQL. When two entries have the same length, you can sort them alphabetically using the `ASC` or `DESC` clause as needed.

Here is the SQL query:

```sql
SELECT title, director
FROM movies
ORDER BY LENGTH(title) DESC, title ASC,
         LENGTH(director) DESC, director ASC;
```

This query will sort the `title` column by its character length in descending order. If two titles have the same length, they will be sorted alphabetically in ascending order. The same logic applies to the `director` column.

A palindrome in this response is: **"madam"**.
